commitResults.messageBody:
- #220: state payment status is case-insensitive in parameter table
- #241: clarify email must not be blank in parameter table
- #246: add planned enhancement noting edit no-op is a future tweak

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: require at least one prefix for find command (#259)
commitResults.messageBody:
- find S (no prefix) now throws MESSAGE_USAGE instead of silently
  treating the preamble as a name search
- Update FindCommandParserTest: replace parse_validArgs_returnsFindCommand
  with parse_noPrefixArgs_throwsParseException covering bare keywords

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: detect malformed prefix with space before slash in find (#235)
commitResults.messageBody:
- find ps /Due now throws a clear error instead of returning empty list
- Root cause: ArgumentTokenizer misses 'ps /' so it lands in preamble;
  detect any preamble containing '/' and reject with a helpful message
- Add parse_malformedPrefix_throwsParseException test

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: widen EmergencyContact to accept landlines and short codes (#233 #224)
commitResults.messageBody:
- Change VALIDATION_REGEX from [89]\d{7} to \d{3,15}
- Update MESSAGE_CONSTRAINTS to reflect new valid range
- Update UG parameter table for ec/ to match new constraint
- Update EmergencyContactTest: remove old SG-mobile-only cases,
  add short code (999), landline (61234567), and length boundary tests

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: require at least one prefix for find command (#259)

  with parse_noPrefixArgs_throwsParseException covering bare keywords

commitResults.messageBody: fix: detect malformed prefix with space before slash in find

Fix #218: Use consistent lowercase `tutorcentral.jar` in Notes for
Users to match the Quick Start and Gradle jar output.

Fix #219: Update the Features `add` example to use `John Tan` so it
matches the "Example result after adding a student" screenshot.

#238 and #239 (add example errors) are already resolved by the
LessonSlot triplet refactor in f7e7fab1 — both examples now parse
cleanly through AddCommandParser with matching s/d/ti counts and
valid 24-hour times. No further change needed; closing those issues
as stale once this PR merges.

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: surface field names in duplicate-prefix error (#268)
commitResults.messageBody:
Rewrite Messages.getErrorMessageForDuplicatePrefixes to map each
Prefix to a human-readable field label ("Name" instead of "n/"), so
users see "Multiple values specified for the following single-valued
field(s): Name" rather than a raw prefix token.

Switch the underlying collection from HashSet to LinkedHashSet so
the ordering now matches the caller's verifyNoDuplicatePrefixesFor
argument order. Update two existing parser tests that relied on
HashSet's coincidental ordering to use the deterministic parser
order instead.

Confirmed MESSAGE_INVALID_PERSON_DISPLAYED_INDEX already exists at
Messages.java:17, so no work is needed for the #255 shared-constant
dependency — Abhishek can import it directly.

Fixes #268

commitResults.messageTitle: test: add regression tests for blank-subject edit rejection (#242)
commitResults.messageBody:
The current parser already correctly rejects blank subjects through
ParserUtil.parseSubject validation. This commit adds three regression
tests to lock down that behaviour:
- blank subject with day and time (tester's exact reproduction)
- whitespace-only subject
- blank subject in a second lesson slot

No main source changes required — the feature-freeze LoC impact is 0.

Fixes #242

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: allow hyphens in student names (#225)
commitResults.messageBody:
Add hyphen to Name.VALIDATION_REGEX so names like "Mary-Jane" and
"Anne-Marie" are accepted, matching real-world naming conventions.
Leading hyphens are still rejected.

Fixes #225

Update Tag.equals to use equalsIgnoreCase and Tag.hashCode to use
toLowerCase so that "Friend" and "friend" are treated as the same
tag. This prevents duplicate tags that differ only in casing.

Fixes #270

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: reject duplicate and overlapping lesson slots (#222 #236 #232 #256)
commitResults.messageBody:
Extend ParserUtil.parseLessonSlots to detect:
1. Exact duplicates (same subject+day+time) via HashSet dedup.
2. Day+time overlaps (different subjects at the same day+time) via a
   HashMap keyed on "day|time".

Both add and edit commands pass through this method, so a single
change covers all entry points.

Fixes #222
Fixes #236
Fixes #232
Fixes #256

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: show usage error for mark with multiple indices (#237)
commitResults.messageBody:
Detect multi-token preamble in MarkCommandParser before parseIndex
so that `mark 1 2 ps/Due` shows the correct usage message instead
of a confusing "Index is not a non-zero unsigned integer" error.

Fixes #237

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: guard against null keys in attendance serialization (#243)
commitResults.messageBody:
Filter out null subject keys and null lesson keys in
JsonAdaptedPerson's source constructor so that Jackson does not
throw "Null key for a Map" when serializing corrupted attendance
records to JSON. Null entries are silently dropped.

Fixes #243

commitResults.messageTitle: test: cover null lesson-key and null status branches in JsonAdaptedPerson
commitResults.messageBody:
Add tests for the remaining null-guard branches in the attendance
serialization: null inner-map key and null AttendanceStatus value.
Improves JsonAdaptedPerson branch coverage from 92% to 96%.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: address PR review comments and fix UG formatting
commitResults.messageBody:
Incorporate Dayne's review feedback on the UG. Also ensure the #218
jar filename casing fix (tutorcentral.jar) is applied consistently.

commitResults.messageTitle: test: clean up test comments per review feedback
commitResults.messageBody:
Remove issue number and tester reference from test comments; describe
the equivalence partition instead.

commitResults.messageTitle: refactor: use more descriptive variable names in parseLessonSlots
commitResults.messageBody:
Rename result -> lessonSlots, seen -> seenLessonSlots,
byDayTime -> slotsByDayTime, existing -> conflictingSlot per review.

commitResults.messageTitle: feat: add AttendanceRecordKey infrastructure for lesson-based attendance (#251)
commitResults.messageBody:
Add AttendanceRecordKey utility class with of(), getLessonSlotKey(), and
isValid() to support the new "Day Time - Lesson" attendance key format.
Update Lesson regex to allow hyphens for date labels, add parseLessonName
to ParserUtil, switch JsonAdaptedPerson to use AttendanceRecordKey.isValid(),
and fix EditCommand pruning to extract slot keys from full record keys.

commitResults.messageTitle: feat: add remark support to add command and refresh UG screenshots (#227)
commitResults.messageBody:
Parse optional r/REMARK prefix in AddCommandParser so users can set
a remark when adding a student. Update UG docs and regenerate all
screenshots to reflect current app state.

commitResults.messageTitle: test: add coverage for parseLessonName, AttendanceRecordKey, and EditCommand pruning
commitResults.messageBody:
Cover the uncovered parseLessonName method in ParserUtil, the
parts.length != 2 branch in AttendanceRecordKey.isValid, and the
inner canonicalSubject == null path in EditCommand.pruneAttendanceRecords.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: update guides and diagrams for attendance l/LESSON change (#251)
commitResults.messageBody:
Update MarkAttendanceSequenceDiagram to include lesson parameter.
Update DG markattendance implementation details, attendance key format,
and add planned enhancements #13 (lesson end times) and #14 (date
validation for l/LESSON). Update UG markattendance format, examples,
and command summary table.

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix issue 261: Remove intermediate system-display steps from Use Cases 03 and 04
commitResults.messageBody:
- Remove 'Tutor Central shows the required details to be input' from Use Case 03
- Remove 'Tutor Central shows the details that can be updated' from Use Case 04
- Update step numbers in extensions accordingly
- Align use cases with actual CLI workflow where users provide full commands directly

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix issue 255: Standardize error messages for out of range index
commitResults.messageBody:
- Replace custom MESSAGE_OUT_OF_RANGE in DeleteCommand with Messages.MESSAGE_INVALID_PERSON_DISPLAYED_INDEX
- Update DeleteCommand and EditCommand to use consistent error message format
- Fix test files to expect the standardized error message
- Remove trailing whitespace to pass checkstyle

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix issue 248: Preserve subject case in attendance records
commitResults.messageBody:
- Remove toLowerCase() normalization in EditCommand.pruneAttendanceRecords
- Preserve original subject casing from lesson slots when storing attendance
- Ensures attendance display shows correct subject case (Chemistry vs chemistry)
- Maintains consistency between MarkAttendanceCommand and EditCommand behavior

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix issue 247: Make name comparison case-insensitive in isSamePerson
commitResults.messageBody:
- Change Person.isSamePerson() to use equalsIgnoreCase() instead of equals()
- Prevents adding duplicate students with different case variations (e.g., 'Doe' vs 'doe')
- Ensures uniqueness check is case-insensitive while maintaining case-sensitive full equality

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix issue 230: Add email uniqueness validation for students
commitResults.messageBody:
- Add hasSameEmail() method to Person class for email comparison
- Update UniquePersonList.contains() to check both name and email uniqueness
- Update PersonTest to reflect case-insensitive name comparison behavior
- Prevents adding students with duplicate email addresses
- Maintains name-based identity while enforcing email uniqueness

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix issue 221: Enhance markattendance to support multiple students
commitResults.messageBody:
- Add support for multiple student indices separated by commas
- Update command usage examples to show both single and multiple index syntax
- Maintain backward compatibility with single index usage
- Improve efficiency for marking attendance for entire class at once
- Update parser to handle comma-separated index parsing
- Fix tests to reflect new multiple index functionality

commitResults.messageTitle: Update PersonTest for case-insensitive name comparison
commitResults.messageBody:
- Fix test to expect case-insensitive behavior in isSamePerson method
- Update test case for VALID_NAME_BOB.toLowerCase() to return true instead of false
- Aligns with issue 247 fix for preventing duplicate names with different cases

commitResults.messageBody:

- Document support for multiple categorized remarks per student
- Include detailed features: categorization, filtering, search, chronological history
- Addresses tutor need for different remarks per subject/context

- Update failing EditCommand tests to use unique identifiers instead of conflicting BOB constants
- Fix execute_editNameOnly_preservesAttendanceRecords test by editing remark instead of name
- Resolves DuplicatePersonException in test suite caused by conflicts with existing typical persons
Fix checkstyle violation by removing unused import from EditCommandTest.java

commitResults.messageTitle: Implement l/LESSON parameter for markattendance command
commitResults.messageBody:
- Add PREFIX_LESSON to CliSyntax.java
- Update MarkAttendanceCommandParser to parse l/ parameter
- Update MarkAttendanceCommand to use lesson label in attendance key
- Update success messages to include lesson
- Update equals method to compare lesson field
- Fix all test files to use new constructor signature
- Fix checkstyle line length violations
- Update integration tests to include l/LESSON parameter

This allows distinguishing attendance for different lesson sessions
(e.g., Week 1 vs Week 2) for the same day and time slot.

Fix: l/LESSON parameter for markattendance command

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Add documentation for multiple indices support in markattendance command
commitResults.messageBody:
- Update User Guide to document batch attendance marking with comma-separated indices
- Add examples for single vs multiple student operations
- Update command summary table with new syntax format
- Update Developer Guide implementation section to reflect multiple indices architecture
- Fix critical documentation gap where implemented feature was undocumented

Resolves issue where markattendance supported multiple indices (1,2,3) but users
were unaware of this batch operation capability.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Fix critical documentation inconsistencies and validation rules
commitResults.messageBody:
- Update name validation to include hyphens (fixes issue with hyphenated names)
- Update Use Case 09 to include multiple students attendance marking
- Clarify day validation to explicitly mention 3-letter abbreviations
- Remove incorrect space handling claims from tag validation
- Ensure all parameter constraints match actual implementation

These fixes resolve critical documentation gaps where users could encounter
unexpected validation errors when using documented features.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Fix all documented contradictions and mismatches
commitResults.messageBody:
- Fix attendance granularity contradiction in UG (records stored per slot + lesson)
- Remove stale planned enhancement for remarks during creation (already implemented)
- Fix NFR duplicate student criteria (email-based, case-insensitive, not name-based)
- Fix planned enhancements numbering (renumber items 12->11, 14->12)
- Standardize branding to TutorCentral (no space) across DG and MainWindow.fxml

All issues identified in user analysis have been resolved.
docs: Add documentation for multiple indices support

commitResults.messageBody:
- Fix confusing [|INDEX1,INDEX2,...] notation for multiple indices in markattendance command

- Add Edge Cases and Special Scenarios section covering common user scenarios
- Standardize formatting consistency across all examples (e.g., vs e.g.)
- Fix command summary table formatting issue
- Update emergency contact field documentation to reflect 3-15 digit validation

Improves documentation clarity, user guidance, and troubleshooting support.
